title: Move OLA adapter invariants into Rust
goal: Plan and implement the adapter-language repair so route-key sanitizing, reward clamping and state normalization are covered by the tested Rust core instead of living primarily in Python scripts.

acceptance.assertion: Route-key sanitizing, reward clamping and state normalization are represented in Rust with regression coverage.
Python OLA scripts are reduced to fixture, probe or delegation roles rather than owning the transformation contract.

metadata.claude_delegation: primary architecture pass before code changes; implementation may then route through Grabowski/Codex
metadata.superseded_reason: PR #194 overlapped OPERATOR-LEARNING-AXIS-V1-T003, remained red on build-test, and was closed after PR #195 merged the broader Rust-owned OLA invariant surface.

validated_acceptance.rust-invariants: Heimlern PR #195 adds heimlern_core::ola with Rust tests for route-key sanitizing, fail-closed route-key errors, reward clamping, state normalization, outcome conversion and decision-outcome conversion.
validated_acceptance.python-degraded: scripts/ola_adapter.py becomes a compatibility wrapper around heimlern-ola, and scripts/ola_probe.py delegates route-key derivation to Rust-owned normalization instead of owning the transformation contract.

validation: GitHub checks for PR #195 passed: Analyze actions/python/rust, CodeQL, build-test, repo-root, schema-validate, templates.
Local before merge: py_compile for ola_adapter.py, ola_probe.py and validate_weight_adjustment_coherence.py passed.
Local before merge: cargo test -q -p heimlern-core ola:: passed.
Local before merge: cargo test -q -p heimlern-cli --bin heimlern-ola passed.
Local before merge: cargo test -q -p heimlern-feedback passed.
Local before merge: python3 scripts/ola_adapter.py --self-test and python3 scripts/ola_probe.py --self-test passed.
Local before merge: validate_weight_adjustment_coherence.py passed against pinned metarepo policy.weight_adjustment.v1 schema.
Post-merge validation on Heimlern origin/main 8453c2c5332610dacf7920f5f8795b0bdcf8b6b4 repeated the same focused Python, Rust, schema and diff checks successfully.
